retry_reason: The caller must hold a valid reference before calling; double-free would occur if refcount already zero, so retry is a bug.
contract_summary: This is a reference-release (put) function. It decrements the reference count of a bpf_crypto_ctx. If the count reaches zero, it schedules the underlying object for RCU-free via call_rcu. The caller must hold a valid reference before calling this function and must not use the object after the call (unless they have another reference). No additional locking or RCU read-side is required by the caller for this function itself.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement the reference count and free the object on last reference; no other logic exists.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test(&ctx->usage) is a put operation on the parameter ctx. The access path ctx.usage.refs correctly traces from struct bpf_crypto_ctx to its field usage (refcount_t) and then to its internal atomic_t refs. The type chain bpf_crypto_ctx--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the struct definitions. Segment counts (3 segments, 3 types) are equal.
